MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO REQUEST FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION (TRANSACTION ID # 62515238) FILED BY PLAINTIFF WOODRUFF-SAWYER & CO.

LAW AND MOTION, PLAINTIFF WOODRUFF SAWYER'S MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS AND WHILE THERE ARE SOME HARM ON WOODRUFF SAWYER IN THE DENIAL OF THIS MOTION, THIS WOULD CAUSE EXTREME HARM TO MR. REITER IN GRANTING THIS MOTION AND THE BALANCE OF THE HARM WEIGHS IN FAVOR OF DENYING THE MOTION AS DOES THE LACK OF LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. THE TRO ISSUED BY THE JUDGE IN MARIN COUNTY IS DISSOLVED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 =302/HK

Plaintiff Woodruff Sawyer And Cos Update To The Court Regarding Pertinent Case Activity And Resubmission Of Papers Supporting Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Sandra Rappaport of Hanson brdigett, Yel. No. 1-415-777-3200 appearing for the
Plaintiff;
Atty. Mike Margolis of Blank Rome,appearing for the Defendant Lockton;
Atty. Robert K. Carrol of Arent Fox, Tel. No. 1-415-994-6330 appearing for
the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiff Woodruff Sawyer And Cost Update To The Court Regarding Pertinent Case Activity And Resubmission Of Papers Supporting Preliminary Injunction - The matter was argued regarding 1) whether the language of 16601 covers a situation where an employee receives stock in his employer after the employee starts his employment and the receipt of stock is conditioned on the employee's agreeing to a covenant not to compete and 2) there is any case law holding that 16601 applies or does not apply to a situation where an employee receives stock in his employer after the employee starts his employment and the receipt of stock is conditioned on the employee's agreeing to a covenant not to compete.

The court ruled as follows: Plaintiff Woodruff Sawyer's Motion for a preliminary Injunction is Denied on the grounds that the Plaintiff has not shown a likelihood of success on the merits and while there are some harm on Woodruff Sawyer in the denial of this motion, this would cause extreme harm to Mr. Reiter in granting this motion and the balance of the harm weighs in favor of denying the motion as does the lack of likelihood of success on the merits. The TRO issued by the judge in Marin county is dissolved. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 =302/hk
